stomach_abdomen.text: Atonic dyspepsia, with gas and acidity. Heart-burn and gastralgia. Regurgitation of food. Abdomen feels empty and retracted. Dyspepsia after infectious diseases; especially grip, dysentery. Tongue dry or with brown sticky coating. Cirrhosis of liver with ascites

urinary.text: Excessive desire-impossible to retain urine; copious micturition day and night. As soon as the child wakes up the bed is drenched

extremities.text: Inclination to yawn and stretch (Rhus). Sensation of coldness over back. Prostration, with hunger. Cold extremities, with sensation of internal coldness (Helo-derma)

dosing.notes: First to third potency, or spoonful doses of Aqua Quassiae

keynotes.text: color="#800000">QUASSIA AMARA Quassia-wood (QUASSIA - PICRAENA EXCELSA) Acts on gastric organs as a tonic (Gentian; Hydr). Seems to possess marked action on eyes, producing amblyopia and cataract. Pain in right intercostal muscles above the liver. Pressure and stitches in liver, and sympathetically in spleen.
